Sunday Racing: Freedman festival in Canberra

The Australian Turf Club’s Supreme Court fight with Racing NSW is shaping up as one of the most significant court tests of Racing NSW’s administrator power over race clubs. The case was argued over three days in late February, with the Court’s decision expected in the coming weeks. This article walks through the case being run on both sides, the questions the judge is being asked to answer, and the funding structure that keeps surfacing beneath it.

Bought for $100,000 from Vinery Stud, Russian Revolution filly Lara Antipova is now an unbeaten Group 1-winning juvenile with over NZ$550,000 in the bank. She is being aimed at the G1 Coolmore Stud Stakes next spring.

Two Breeders’ Cup victors were out on Saturday with Splendora making it five wins in succession with a G1 Beholder Stakes victory, while last season’s Champion 3-year-old filly Nitrogen was third in a thrilling Azeri Stakes. Justify added a new Grade 1 winner in the G1 Santa Anita Handicap with British Isles.

After a week of discussion over weights, it was Bjorn Baker's Caballus who prevailed in the G1 Newmarket Handicap to secure his first top flight win, while James McDonald rode a six-timer on the Randwick card including the G1 Canterbury Stakes with Joliestar.

The 3-year-old Group 1s on both sides of the Tasman were thrilling events thanks to the domination of Sheza Alibi at Randwick and the dramatic finish to Road To Paris's G1 New Zealand Derby. Well Written kept her unbeaten record intact in the R. Listed NZB Kiwi and Beadman was all class in the Listed Fireball.

The Golden Slipper picture became more diffuse after Saturday with Paradoxium returning to form in the Todman, while Chayan, Grinzinger Heart, and Medicinal all put up their hands for the Slipper. In New Zealand, unbeaten G1 Sistema Stakes winner Lara Antipova will be set for the G1 Coolmore Stud Stakes in the spring.

Four Group 1s across Randwick and Flemington headline Saturday's racing, but the fight for a place at the G1 Golden Slipper Stakes start line rages on at both tracks too. The trainers of Stefi Magnetica, Antino, and Rivellino was keen to not have their charges written off pre-race, while the Blue Army will attempt another feature sweep.

The widespread weight allotments for the G1 Newmarket Handicap on Saturday have understandably been a hot topic of conversation all week, particularly the 57kg given to boom colt Tentyris. Everyone believes it's a swing in their direction, but co-trainer Sam Freedman has full faith in his stable star.
